center.name: 西高止山脉
center.summary: 西高止山脉是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联19种鸟类，包括灰头林鸽、White-rumped spinetail、Jungle owlet等19种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in the Western Ghats in southern Kerala and southern Tamil Nadu

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Jerdon|Jerdon]], 1839) | range_map=RhopocichlaAtriceps.svg | synonyms = }} The dark-fronted babbler (Dumetia atriceps) is an Old World babbler found in the Western Ghats of India and the forests of Sri Lanka

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: There are two subspecies, with that found in the Western Ghats sometimes considered a full species

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |year=1847 |journal=Journal of the Asiatic Society of Bengal |volume=16 |issue=1 |pages=385–387 |url=https://archive.org/details/journalofasiatic161asia/page/386 |title=Report of the Curator, Museum of Zoology}} | range_map=MegalaimaMalabaricaMap.png | synonyms = Bucco malabaricus, Xantholaema malabarica, Megalaima malabarica }} The Malabar barbet (Psilopogon malabaricus) is an Asian barbet native to the Western Ghats in India. It was formerly treated as a race of the crimson-fronted barbet (Psilopogon rubricapillus)

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In India, it is mainly found in the Western Ghats, hill forests of central India and in parts of the Eastern Ghats

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: roonwali, with female on right | image2 =Ceylon frogmouth.ogg | image2_caption = Calls | genus = Batrachostomus | species = moniliger | authority = Blyth, 1849 }} The Sri Lanka frogmouth, Sri Lankan frogmouth or Ceylon frogmouth (Batrachostomus moniliger) is a small frogmouth found in the Western Ghats of south India and Sri Lanka

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is very similar to the more widespread brown-headed barbet (or large green barbet, Psilopogon zeylanicus), but this species has a distinctive supercilium and a broad white cheek stripe below the eye and is found in the forest areas of the Western Ghats, parts of the Eastern Ghats and adjoining hills

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The white-rumped spinetail or white-rumped needletail (Zoonavena sylvatica) is a species of swift found in the forests of Bangladesh, India (the Western Ghats) and Nepal

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |last13=Lei |first13=Fumin |last14=Olsson |first14=Urban |year=2018 |title=Complete species-level phylogeny of the leaf warbler (Aves: Phylloscopidae) radiation |url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/S1055790317308783 |journal=Molecular Phylogenetics and Evolution |language=en |volume=126 |pages=141–152 |doi=10.1016/j.ympev.2018.03.031|pmid=29631054 |s2cid=4720300 }} |cladogram=}} Breeding in central China and the Himalayas, it winters in Sri Lanka, the Western Ghats and associated hill ranges

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The bronzed drongo is found in the Western Ghats and Eastern Ghats of India and the lower Himalayas from western Uttaranchal eastwards into Indochina and Hainan, the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra and northern Borneo

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Race perniger (Hodgson, 1836) is found in the Himalayan foothills west through Nepal into the Indian states of Himachal Pradesh and Jammu and Kashmir, and in the forests of the Eastern and Western Ghats in peninsular India and Sri Lanka.{{cite journal|author=Prater, S.H.|year=1940|title=The Indian Black Eagle (Ictinaetus malayanus perniger Hodgs.) in Salsette|url=https://biodiversitylibrary.org/page/47819479|journal=J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The rufous woodpecker (Micropternus brachyurus) is a medium-sized brown woodpecker native to South and Southeast Asia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is migratory, breeding in the forests along the Himalayas of Nepal, India, Myanmar and Bangladesh. They winter in the hill forests of the Western Ghats of India and in Sri Lanka

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Jerdon|Jerdon]], 1839) | synonyms = Turdoides subrufusLayardia subrufaTimalia subrufaTurdoides subrufaTimalia poecilorhyncha }} The rufous babbler (Argya subrufa) is an endemic species of bird found in the Western Ghats of southern India of the family Leiothrichidae It is dark brown and long tailed, and is usually seen foraging in noisy groups along open hillsides with a mixture of grass, bracken and forest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Nilgiri wood pigeon (Columba elphinstonii) is a large pigeon found in the moist deciduous forests and sholas of the Western Ghats in southwestern India

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in shola habitat in a small section of the Western Ghats in southwestern Karnataka and northern Kerala

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in the forests of the Western Ghats mainly south of Goa.{{cite journal|doi=10.1073/pnas.89.12.5311|author=Daniels, R J Ranjit|author2=NV Joshi|author3=Madhav Gadgil|name-list-style=amp |year=1992 |title= On the relationship between bird and woody plant species diversity in the Uttara Kannada district of south India|journal= Proc

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The blue-throated flycatcher is found in much of the Indian subcontinent, all through the Himalayas, the plains and Western Ghats of India in the cold months, and also extends eastwards into Bangladesh, and to Arakan and the Tenasserim Hills in Myanmar

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The golden-headed cisticola (Cisticola exilis), also known as the bright-capped cisticola, is a species of warbler in the family Cisticolidae, found in Australia and thirteen Asian countries
